sections.heading: Worker Identification & Skills
sections.body:
A worker needs a Name and an ID. Their abilities are defined by Skills, graded on a basic (1) to master (10) scale.
* Example: A worker with 'Bartender: 7' can fulfill any bartending requirement of level 7 or lower.

sections.heading: Availability Windows
sections.body:
Time windows define exactly when a worker can be scheduled, formatted in 24-hour time (e.g., 08:00-16:00). Outside these windows, they cannot be assigned.
* Example: '09:00-17:00' means the worker is strictly unavailable at 17:01.

sections.heading: Preferences & Limits
sections.body:
Preferences (HIGH/LOW) impact the solver's score but do not prevent assignments. Min/Max hours define their workload boundaries across the scheduling period.
* Example: Assigning a 'LOW' preference shift reduces the overall schedule quality score, but is still allowed if no other worker is available.

sections.heading: Defining the Shift
sections.body: Provide a Shift Name, Day of the Week, and Time boundaries using standard 24-hour format (e.g., 09:00-17:00).

sections.heading: The Staffing Hierarchy
sections.body:
• **Shifts** contain multiple **Tasks**.
• **Tasks** contain **Options** (different ways to staff).
• **Options** contain **Requirements** (the actual needs).
* Example: A 'Service' Task might have an Option A (needs 2 Waiters) OR Option B (needs 1 Waiter + 1 Runner).

sections.heading: Requirements
sections.body:
A requirement dictates the exact number of workers needed, the required skill, and the *minimum* skill level (1-10).
* Example: A requirement of [Chef:5] x 2 means you need two workers who possess the 'Chef' skill at level 5 or higher.

sections.heading: Hard vs. Soft Constraints
sections.body:
A **Hard Constraint** strictly MUST be met. If it cannot be satisfied, the solver fails and generates no schedule. A **Soft Constraint** is a preference. Breaking it lowers the objective score (penalty), but the schedule still runs.
* Example: A Hard 'Max Hours' constraint means a worker cannot physically be scheduled for a 41st hour.

sections.heading: Available Constraints (Exhaustive List)
sections.body:
• **Max Hours:** Prevents a worker from exceeding a set weekly hour limit (e.g., 40 hours).
• **Minimum Rest:** Ensures a worker gets adequate time off between shifts (e.g., 10 hours between close and open).
• **Mutual Exclusion:** Prevents Worker A and Worker B from ever working the exact same shift together.
• **Co-location:** Forces Worker A and Worker B to ALWAYS work the same shifts together.
• **Task Priorities:** Biases the solver to prefer Option A over Option B when satisfying a task.

topics.title: Schedule: Reading Results and Quality Signals

sections.heading: Assignment Grid
sections.body:
The schedule is built with Days as columns and Shifts as individual cards. Inside each shift card, workers are grouped by the specific Task they are fulfilling.
* Example: Tuesday column -> Evening Shift card -> Kitchen Task -> lists 'John' and 'Mary'.

sections.heading: Interpreting Scores (Soft Violations)
sections.body:
Green scores mean worker preferences were met. Red scores mean Soft Constraints were violated, incurring a penalty that lowers total schedule quality.
* Example: A red '-5' score might mean John was scheduled during a time he marked as 'Avoid', breaking a preference.

sections.heading: Running Diagnostics
sections.body:
If the solver fails to generate a schedule, the grid will remain empty. Click the **Run Diagnostics** button to have the engine explain exactly which Hard Constraint failed.
* Example: Diagnostics output "SKILL GAP: No workers have the 'Chef' skill" if you required a Chef but didn't add one to your Workers list.

sections.heading: Saving Your State
sections.body: Exporting an Excel State File is the official way to safely back up your Workers, Shifts, and Constraints offline.

sections.heading: The Ideal Workflow
sections.body:
1. Edit your Workers and Shifts directly in this UI.
2. Click 'Export State' to download your progress.
3. Later, upload that exact Excel file to restore your work.
* Example: You finish drafting Monday-Wednesday, Export State, and upload it on Friday to finish the week.

sections.heading: ⚠️ CRUCIAL WARNING REGARDING MANUAL EDITS
sections.body:
We strongly advise against manually editing the exported Excel file in Excel or Google Sheets.
* Example: Adding a rogue space or changing a time format manually in Excel will corrupt the syntax and prevent you from successfully re-importing the file later.
